title: Runtime cost snapshot ignores untracked source files
reasoning: The runtime allows implementer changes to non-test source files, including untracked additions, but `snapshotWorktree` builds both baseline and post-implementation maps with `git ls-files` only. Untracked source files created by an implementer are therefore omitted from the after snapshot unless they were staged, so `editCost` will not charge their tokens or regions. A benchmark that passes by adding `src/new.ts` can be reported as zero or heavily undercounted cost, corrupting Vhat.
recommendation: Include untracked, non-ignored source files in the post-implementation snapshot, for example by using `git ls-files -z --cached --others --exclude-standard -- <srcDir>` or equivalent, while preserving deletion handling for tracked files.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The pure `editCost` tests cover added files when the maps already contain them, but the runtime benchmark test modifies only an existing tracked `src/index.ts`; it never exercises an implementer-created untracked source file.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a runtime benchmark where the scripted implementer writes a new `src/added.ts`, the acceptance command requires that file/content, and the resulting artifact must report a nonzero edit cost including the added file.
minimumFixScope: Update runtime source snapshot collection and add one runtime regression test for untracked source additions.
